void_v0.doc: Sum of balances held by approved pools \xe2\x80\x94 drives L.
void_v0.name: TotalMass

void_v0.doc: Sum of ALL outstanding balances \xe2\x80\x94 enforces MAX_SUPPLY.\nDistinct from TotalMass: `TotalSupply` includes user\nbalances and never double-counts a mint.
void_v0.name: TotalSupply

function_v0.doc:
Burn tokens from caller's own balance.
Vaulted accounts CANNOT burn (no exit route).

Lazy evaluation applies here too: if the protocol grew past
the caller's balance between when they were vaulted and now,
the first read re-syncs the flag and the burn proceeds. If
they remain genuinely over L, the burn is blocked \xe2\x80\x94 burning
would be a sneaky exit path (destroy HITZ to avoid sacrifice).
function_v0.name: burn

function_v0.doc:
Mint new HITZ tokens. Only callable by admin.

TWO HARD GUARDS:
1. Supply cap \xe2\x80\x94 refuses any mint that would push
`TotalSupply` past `MAX_SUPPLY` (100,000,000 HITZ).
This closes the historical "blank check" where mint
had no upper bound.
2. Integrity \xe2\x80\x94 if the recipient is a registered pool /
router, its on-chain WASM must still match what was
approved; a bytecode swap aborts the mint before any
balance write.

"Roach Motel" semantics: within those guards, mint never
fails due to vault logic. If the recipient breaches L they
are silently vaulted.
function_v0.name: mint

function_v0.doc: SEP-41 approve: set allowance for a spender with expiration.
function_v0.name: approve

function_v0.doc:
Check if an address is registered as an approved pool.

This is a pure read \xe2\x80\x94 it does NOT run the WASM integrity check.
Use `pool_wasm_hash` to fetch the bound hash, or call any
state-changing transfer / burn to trigger the enforced check.
function_v0.name: is_pool

function_v0.doc: Upgrade the contract WASM in-place. Admin-only.\n\nTEMPORARY \xe2\x80\x94 to be removed before mainnet immutable deployment.
function_v0.name: upgrade

function_v0.doc: SEP-41 allowance: returns current allowance (0 if expired).
function_v0.name: allowance

function_v0.doc: Burn tokens from another account via allowance.\nVaulted accounts CANNOT be burned from (no exit route).
function_v0.name: burn_from

function_v0.doc: Check if an address is registered as an approved router.\nPure read \xe2\x80\x94 see `is_pool` for the integrity-check semantics.
function_v0.name: is_router

function_v0.doc: Transfer admin role to a new address.\nRequires auth from BOTH current admin and new admin.
function_v0.name: set_admin

function_v0.doc: Initialize the Gravity HITZ token with admin, name, symbol.\nDecimals are fixed at 7 (Stellar standard).
function_v0.name: initialize

function_v0.doc:
Enumerate every currently-registered pool.

Authoritative, O(n) in the list size. Clients should prefer this
over event-log scraping + localStorage heuristics \xe2\x80\x94 the list is
updated in lockstep with every `register_pool_address` /
`remove_pool_address` call, so it always reflects the current
on-chain state regardless of RPC event retention windows.
function_v0.name: list_pools

function_v0.doc: The hard-coded supply cap in raw units (7 decimals).\nEqual to 100,000,000 HITZ.
function_v0.name: max_supply

function_v0.doc: Returns the raw TotalMass accumulator (sum of approved pool balances).
function_v0.name: total_mass

event_v0.doc: Emitted exactly once per contract instance, on `initialize`. Lets\nindexers mark the deployment cleanly without polling for the first\nadmin / metadata write.
event_v0.name: InitializedEvent

function_v0.doc: Enumerate every currently-registered router. See `list_pools`.
function_v0.name: list_routers

function_v0.doc: Returns the current Safety Limit L = sqrt(TotalMass \xc3\x97 10^decimals).\nO(1) \xe2\x80\x94 reads the pre-computed accumulator.
function_v0.name: safety_limit

function_v0.doc: Circulating supply \xe2\x80\x94 sum of all outstanding balances.\nAlways \xe2\x89\xa4 `max_supply()`.
function_v0.name: total_supply

event_v0.doc:
Emitted on every successful `set_admin`. The `previous` topic lets
monitors filter on outgoing-from-current-admin to alert on any
transfer of the most powerful role in the contract; the `new` topic
lets the incoming admin's tooling pick up the role transition
without scraping storage diffs.
event_v0.name: AdminChangedEvent

function_v0.doc: SEP-41 transfer_from: delegated transfer via allowance.\nSame Roach Motel rules + lazy evaluation \xe2\x80\x94 vault check on\n`from`, not `spender`.
function_v0.name: transfer_from

function_v0.doc:
Returns the WASM hash the admin bound to `pool` at registration,
or `None` if not registered. Off-chain observers can compare this
to the pool's current on-chain executable to audit integrity
without submitting a fee-bearing transaction.
A returned value of all zeros means "classic account, no
integrity binding".
function_v0.name: pool_wasm_hash

event_v0.doc:
Registration events include the approved WASM hash so off-chain
indexers can snapshot the exact bytecode bound to each address.
On removal the hash emitted is the hash that WAS registered (so
the event log preserves what got unbound), or the zero hash when
the address was never registered (spurious removes), or the zero
hash when the registered address is a non-Wasm (classic) account.
event_v0.name: PoolRegisteredEvent

function_v0.doc: Returns the WASM hash bound to `router` at registration, or None.
function_v0.name: router_wasm_hash

function_v0.doc:
Returns whether an account is currently vaulted.

NOTE: This is a passive read \xe2\x80\x94 it reflects the LAST WRITTEN
flag. If the protocol has grown since the user was trapped,
their real ("lazy-evaluated") status may be "safe" even
though this returns true. The flag will be corrected the
moment they transact. For a canonical realtime answer see
`would_be_vaulted_if(balance)`.
function_v0.name: is_account_vaulted

function_v0.doc:
Canonical "is this account actually stuck right now?" query \xe2\x80\x94
evaluates the physics against their CURRENT balance and the
CURRENT L. Independent of the stored flag.

Returns true iff the account is non-infrastructure and holds
more than L. UIs can call this to decide whether to show the
"vaulted" banner without relying on stale flags.
function_v0.name: is_actually_vaulted

function_v0.doc: Remove an address from the approved pools list. Admin-gated.\nThe pool's current balance is subtracted from TotalMass.
function_v0.name: remove_pool_address

function_v0.doc:
Register an address as an approved pool. Admin-gated.
Pools affect TotalMass and are the ONLY destination vaulted
users can send to (sacrifice mass to widen the road).

The current on-chain WASM hash of `address` is captured at
registration and bound as the approved bytecode \xe2\x80\x94 any later
in-place upgrade of the pool will trip the integrity check.
For classic accounts (no WASM) a zero-hash sentinel is stored
and the integrity check is skipped on subsequent state moves.
Re-registering an existing pool rebinds the current hash \xe2\x80\x94
intended path after a deliberate, reviewed pool upgrade.
function_v0.name: register_pool_address

function_v0.doc: Remove an address from the approved routers list. Admin-gated.
function_v0.name: remove_router_address

function_v0.doc:
Register an address as an approved router. Admin-gated.
Routers are pass-through entities (DEX aggregators, swap
contracts). They NEVER affect TotalMass and vaulted users
CANNOT send to them.

The current on-chain WASM hash of `address` is captured at
registration \xe2\x80\x94 see `register_pool_address` for the integrity
semantics and the non-Wasm fallback.
function_v0.name: register_router_address
